_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to investigate different sources and types of health information and evaluate how reliable and relevant they are to our lives.

_source.w: We are learning to investigate different sources and types of health information and evaluate how reliable and relevant they are to our lives.
y: Health information comes from many sources — and not all of them are trustworthy.
Being able to evaluate health information protects us from misinformation.
Critical health literacy is an essential skill in a world full of competing messages.
eg: Comparing health information from a government website with information from a social media influencer.
Identifying criteria for evaluating health information — source, evidence, purpose.
Investigating who funds health campaigns and whether this affects their message.
Analysing a misleading health claim and identifying why it is unreliable.
Discussing how to find credible health information when we have a question.
Evaluating health apps and websites for accuracy and reliability.
